findings.title: Schema calls target a venue, but the ABI requires an undocumented executeLeg adapter
findings.details: `target` is documented as the enabled venue target for the family, but every adapter dispatches `IRouterLegExecutor(target).executeLeg(...)` rather than a Solidly/Uniswap/Slipstream/Universal Router ABI. A `www` client following the schema and passing the real venue router address plus venue calldata will build routes that satisfy the documented shape but revert at runtime. The contract-side agreement needs to either make `target` an explicit Fame adapter implementing `IRouterLegExecutor` with per-family payload schemas, or replace the generic adapter hook with real venue ABI calls.
findings.suggested_fix: Define the public target/payload contract in the schema and fixtures, including the exact `IRouterLegExecutor` ABI if adapter targets are intended; otherwise implement per-family adapters that consume documented venue calldata.

findings.title: Enum and amount-mode wire values are not frozen in the schema
findings.details: The Solidity ABI encodes `VenueFamily` and `AmountMode` as integer enum ordinals, but the schema only lists display names. It also leaves `BalanceBps` range/denominator and `All` amount handling implicit. That makes the route JSON/TypeScript contract fragile: `www` can choose the wrong uint8, fail to reject `BalanceBps > 10000`, or drift if enum order changes while `version` remains 1.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a canonical wire table for venue and amount-mode ordinals, `BalanceBps` denominator/range/rounding, and ignored `All.amount`; add a fixture or test that asserts the docs/JSON constants match `FameRouterTypes`.

findings.title: RouteExecuted omits route identity needed for client parity and migrations
findings.details: The success event only emits payer, recipient, tokenOut, and output/fee amounts. It omits `version`, `tokenIn`, `amountIn`, `minAmountOutAfterFee`, leg count, and any route hash, so indexers and support tooling cannot prove which client-constructed schema route executed once multiple route builders or a future version exist.
findings.suggested_fix: Emit a stable route identifier such as `keccak256(abi.encode(route))` plus version/input amount, or document that no event-level route parity/audit guarantee exists for v1.

residual_risks: Production Base fixtures are still placeholders, so the real per-venue payload contract cannot be validated for `www` yet.
The schema version is enforced by calldata but is not exposed as a public getter, leaving clients to hardcode or parse docs/ABI artifacts.
testing_gaps: No fixture asserts the documented schema wire values against the Solidity enum ordinals and constants.
No test proves a `www`-constructed fixture route can be encoded from JSON, submitted through the ABI, and matched back to an emitted route identity.
